description: Sector overlay for airlines, aviation operators, airports, ground handlers, maintenance organizations, training organizations, and aviation service providers in Canada.

metadata.scope: Canada sector overlay controls for Aviation. Intended to be used with canada_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official Canadian federal/provincial/territorial legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Transport Canada safety management systems guidance

rules.title: Canadian aviation document, certificate, or approval evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Transport Canada aviation documents, operator certificates, approvals, permits, or authorizations.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Safety management system evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for aviation SMS, hazard identification, risk management, training, corrective action, or internal safety reporting evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Aviation security or restricted-area control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for aviation security, restricted area, screening, access control, or security clearance evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Air passenger protection disclosure evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for APPR-style passenger rights, delay/cancellation, denied boarding, baggage, communication, or compensation disclosure evidence.
